meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to build user onboarding to overcome no project ideas. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first portfolio app.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Build a robust user onboarding flow for any simple SaaS. This project teaches state management, database schemas, and UX psychology—the three pillars of professional software engineering. By mastering onboarding, you create a high-value portfolio piece that proves you understand the complete user lifecycle.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking like a metronome counting down your wasted potential. The 'no project ideas' paralysis is the silent killer of every promising developer's career. You don't need a groundbreaking startup concept; you need a technical challenge that mirrors real-world production demands.

User onboarding is the unsung hero of SaaS—it is the bridge between a visitor and a loyal user. By building an intelligent, multi-step onboarding flow, you aren't just writing code; you are solving the exact problem companies pay six-figure salaries to fix. Let’s turn that blank screen into a functional, impressive asset.

steps.title: Define the User Journey Map
steps.description: Before coding, map out the friction points. Identify what data you need to capture to provide immediate value to the user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a 3-step user onboarding flow schema. Step 1: User profile basics. Step 2: Goal selection. Step 3: Tool configuration. Include a JSON structure for storing this progress in Supabase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a schema.prisma file with a User model containing onboarding_step and preferences fields.'

steps.title: Build the State Machine
steps.description: Onboarding is essentially a state machine. You need to track exactly where the user is to prevent data loss on refresh.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React hook useOnboarding that manages state transitions between steps and syncs with local storage as a fallback.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement a state-managed onboarding component that persists progress to localStorage.'

steps.title: Implement Intelligent Validation
steps.description: Don't just collect data; validate it in real-time to ensure your database stays clean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Zod validation schemas for a 3-step onboarding form. Ensure fields are required and formatted correctly before allowing the 'Next' button to trigger.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate Zod schemas for user onboarding and integrate them into the form submission logic.'

steps.title: Create the UX Feedback Loop
steps.description: Add visual cues like progress bars or success animations to keep the user engaged throughout the process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a progress bar component in Tailwind CSS that updates based on the current step index in the onboarding flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Add a Tailwind CSS progress bar component that visually tracks the user onboarding completion percentage.'

steps.title: Finalize and Deploy
steps.description: Wrap the flow, handle the final submission, and redirect the user to the app dashboard.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the final redirect logic that sends the user to the dashboard only after the database confirms the onboarding record is complete.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the final submission handler to update the database and redirect the user to /dashboard.'

pro_tips: Always build the 'Skip' button first; forcing users through onboarding without an exit strategy is the fastest way to lose them.
Use optimistic UI updates to make your onboarding feel instantaneous, masking the network latency of your database calls.
Store progress in a 'draft' state in your database so users can return later without losing their progress.
Track onboarding drop-off points using simple analytics to identify which question is causing users to churn.
Treat your onboarding flow as a reusable component library—you will use this exact logic in every future project.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, integrate a real authentication provider like Clerk or NextAuth to gate the experience and secure the user data.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to build professional-grade features that companies actually need. Stop dreaming about the perfect app and start shipping the one that gets you hired. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
